name: food_bank_donor_restrictions
premise: Executive director Patrice Coleman has been offered a three-year donation that would double the operating budget of the city's largest food bank, but the donor insists the funds be used only for households with children and only in neighborhoods with school attendance above a certain threshold. The donor argues this focus rewards families who are trying to stay stable and maximizes measurable impact. Staff members warn that the restrictions would divert food away from elderly residents, undocumented households, and unhoused adults who are already waiting longer each month. If Patrice rejects the gift, two satellite pantries will likely close by winter because inflation has exhausted current reserves. If Patrice accepts, the organization's intake line will become a visible map of who is considered deserving enough to eat.

agents.name: Patrice Coleman
agents.identity: I am the executive director of a food bank that has tried to treat hunger as a shared social failure rather than a test of worthiness. I also know that ideal principles do not automatically keep shelves stocked.
agents.goal: I want to keep the food bank solvent without endorsing a system that sorts hungry people into more and less deserving categories. I need to decide whether partial help with unjust conditions is better than less help with fairer rules.

agents.name: Gavin Price
agents.identity: I am the donor's foundation representative and a former management consultant obsessed with measurable outcomes. I believe constrained giving is still morally valuable if it keeps an organization alive and proves results to future funders.
agents.goal: I want Patrice to accept the restricted gift because serving some households more effectively is better than serving everyone inadequately. I see targeted conditions as responsible stewardship, not discrimination.

choices: I will accept the restricted donation and expand food support for qualifying families, even though other hungry groups will be deprioritized.
I will reject the restricted donation because the food bank should not institutionalize donor-defined deservingness, even if that means closing two pantries.
forced_choice.call_to_action: Patrice Coleman must decide whether to accept the donor's restricted food-bank gift. What does Patrice Coleman do?
